title: Ship Engineers
description: Supervise and coordinate activities of crew engaged in operating and maintaining engines, boilers, deck machinery, and electrical, sanitary, and refrigeration equipment aboard ship.

tasks.text: Monitor engine, machinery, or equipment indicators when vessels are underway, and report abnormalities to appropriate shipboard staff.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Monitor the availability, use, or condition of lifesaving equipment or pollution preventatives to ensure that international regulations are followed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Monitor and test operations of engines or other equipment so that malfunctions and their causes can be identified.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Start engines to propel ships, and regulate engines and power transmissions to control speeds of ships, according to directions from captains or bridge computers.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform or participate in emergency drills, as required.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform general marine vessel maintenance or repair work, such as repairing leaks, finishing interiors, refueling, or maintaining decks.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Maintain or repair engines, electric motors, pumps, winches, or other mechanical or electrical equipment, or assist other crew members with maintenance or repair duties.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain complete records of engineering department activities, including machine operations.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Operate or maintain off-loading liquid pumps or valves.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain electrical power, heating, ventilation, refrigeration, water, or sewerage systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install engine controls, propeller shafts, or propellers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean engine parts and keep engine rooms clean.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record orders for changes in ship speed or direction, and note gauge readings or test data, such as revolutions per minute or voltage output, in engineering logs or bellbooks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Order and receive engine room stores, such as oil or spare parts, maintain inventories, and record usage of supplies.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Act as a liaison between a ship's captain and shore personnel to ensure that schedules and budgets are maintained and that the ship is operated safely and efficiently.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Supervise marine engine technicians engaged in the maintenance or repair of mechanical or electrical marine vessels, and inspect their work to ensure that it is performed properly.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Fabricate engine replacement parts, such as valves, stay rods, or bolts, using metalworking machinery.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
